name: LinkedIn MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with LinkedIn — searching profiles, reading job postings, accessing company information, and retrieving professional network data through MCP tool calls. Supports recruitment workflows, job search automation, and competitive intelligence gathering from LinkedIn's professional network.
use_cases: Searching and filtering LinkedIn profiles for recruitment from talent acquisition agents
Monitoring job postings and requirements from job search agents
Gathering company information and employee counts from research agents
Automating outreach targeting from sales and recruitment agents
not_for: Mass data scraping violating LinkedIn's Terms of Service
Automated connection requests (prohibited by LinkedIn ToS)
Bypassing LinkedIn's rate limits or anti-scraping measures
best_when: A recruiter or sales professional wants AI assistance searching LinkedIn for relevant profiles and job postings — within LinkedIn's acceptable use policies.
avoid_when: You need bulk data extraction or automated messaging — LinkedIn actively enforces ToS against scraping and automation. Risk of account suspension is real.

auth.notes: LinkedIn authentication required. Either OAuth API access (requires LinkedIn partner approval for Marketing API) or session-based auth using browser cookies. Official API access is limited — community MCPs often use cookie-based auth which is ToS-grey area.

pricing.free_tier_limits: LinkedIn free account access; LinkedIn API partner access has separate application
pricing.paid_tiers: LinkedIn Premium required for some search features
pricing.notes: LinkedIn account required. LinkedIn's official API access requires partner approval and is very limited. Community MCP tools often use session auth. LinkedIn Premium ($30-$90/mo) unlocks more search capability.

performance.rate_limits: LinkedIn aggressively rate-limits scraping; session-based access may trigger blocks

known_agent_gotchas: CRITICAL: LinkedIn aggressively blocks automated access — account suspension risk is real
Official LinkedIn API is very restricted — most community MCPs use cookie/session auth (ToS grey area)
LinkedIn frequently changes anti-bot detection — community MCPs may break without warning
GDPR compliance required for EU user data — personal data handling needs legal review
Rate limits are enforced aggressively — agent loops can trigger account restrictions
agent_readiness.security_notes: LinkedIn credentials or session cookies required. Cookie-based auth is fragile and ToS-risky. Do not store credentials insecurely. GDPR applies to European LinkedIn data. Account suspension risk for automated use.

security_score_components.security_notes: LinkedIn credentials at risk if exposed. Cookie-based auth is fragile. GDPR applies to personal data. ToS compliance required — account suspension risk for automated scraping.
